rag_rules: Repair type mismatches by aligning AGREE expressions with the actual AADL feature types, not by adding new architecture.
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' by fixing or removing the first unresolved identifier on the offending line.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers and types visible in the AADL model or declared within the same AGREE annex.
Prefer deleting invalid clauses over introducing new components or bindings when type resolution fails.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool — never use 'integer' or 'boolean'.
Comparison expressions must evaluate to bool; use eq helpers to encapsulate range checks.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not declared in the model or its dependencies.
Do not attempt to repair type errors by modifying unrelated component structures or adding new connections.
